What are Spokane's women going
to do with the ballot, now that they
have been given the right Of fran.
chise.


II would surprise you to know
that most Of them are undecided.


Some Of them think that women
should have the right Of ballot, and
should vote, while others are as
firmly convinced that women have
no business mixing in the politics
Of the nation.


Still more have paid no attention
to the matter, and are not certain
az to their opinion OF woman suf.
frage until they have an opportun'
ity to see how II works out.


In order to determine the atti.
tude of the women of the city on
the suffrage question, The Press
this morning interviewed number
of the best known women of the
city, and was surprised to find that
most of them had paid little or nc
attention to the question.


Mrs. c. NJ. Fassett, wife of the
president of the chamber of com
merce. said that she is undecided
as to whether or rot woman suf
frage will prove good thing. Shs
is of the opinion that woman Cal
find plenty to Occupy .s,y mind y
the sphere of the home, but at thu
same time she believes that 1t II
proper for them to have the righ
of suffrage If they care to exercise
it. Il think" said Mrs. Fassett
that so long as we have been civ
en- the ballot, all good womer
should vote, just the same as should
their husbands, and ! for one will
certainly exercise my franchise"
